name: claude-reflect
description: A self-learning system for Claude Code that captures user corrections and workflow patterns via hooks, then syncs learnings to CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md through a manual review process. Includes SKILL.md, slash commands (/reflect, /reflect-skills, /skip-reflect, /view-queue), and Python hook scripts.

findings_summary.notes: Well-engineered skill with transparent hook usage. Four hooks are registered: UserPromptSubmit (capture_learning.py — reads prompt from stdin, appends to ~/.claude/learnings-queue.json), PreCompact (check_learnings.py), PostToolUse/Bash (post_commit_reminder.py), and SessionStart (session_start_reminder.py). All scripts are readable Python that operate only on local CLAUDE.md/queue files. No network calls, no exfiltration, corrections are queued for human review before being applied. Includes a full test suite (160 passing per README). MIT licensed. SKILL.md is well-structured with clear trigger descriptions. Changelog and distribution docs present. Minor gap: no SECURITY.md. Overall a high-quality, trustworthy skill.
